title:
Spirit 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Runeword
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Spirit is an excellent end-game caster shield and mid-game weapon Runeword. It's fairly inexpensive (considering the skill bonus) and is often rolled many times to get 35% Faster Cast Rate. 
 The only drawback is that the shield version requires a base shield with 4 sockets, and the earliest available (exluding auric shields) is 
 
 Monarch , requiring level 54 and 156 strength . For the sword version a normal 
 
 Crystal Sword suffices. 
 
 Spirit shield is commonly equipped on switch alongside Call To Arms to increase the skill level of oskills, and thus the amount of Life and Mana the player receives when casting 
 
 Battle Orders . 
 
 See Creating a Spirit Sword Early in the Ladder for tips on quickly obtaining Spirit .

Description by Trang Oul

text:
Please note, that since a 4os (4 open socket) item requires ilvl 26+ the best place to farm a base is normal difficulty 
 
 The Secret Cow Level which you can then use the 
 
 Larzuk quest ( 
 
 Siege on Harrogath ) to add sockets which will be maxed at 4. 
 
 The last half of a5 normal can drop the bases, but it's usually the easiest to farm in Norm cows for your bases and since Cow King being killed doesn't prevent you from opening 
 
 Portal to the Secret Cow Level (D2R only) you should have no problem blazing through norm cows to find your base ( 
 
 Targe for paladins can also drop here for another 4os from 
 
 Larzuk - Rush another char to a5 for 
 
 Larzuk quest if needed or use 
 
 Recipe: Add Sockets to Normal Weapon / 
 
 Recipe: Add Sockets to Normal Shield to try and get max sockets).

title:
Spirit 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Runeword
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
It may go without saying, but the +2 To All Skills found on Spirit is the largest skill bonus that can be found on an off-hand item for the Amazon, Druid, and Sorceress. 
 
 Auric Shields (Paladin), Shrunken Heads (Necromancer), 1-handed weapons and 2-handed swords (Barbarian), and Katars (Assassin) can all provide higher bonuses either to All Skills or to all skills of a particular tree.

 Description

title:
Enigma 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Runeword
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Enigma grants the 
 
 Teleport skill to any character regardless of their class, making it a valuable runeword that is useful for many builds. Notably, it isn't used often by Amazons due to their incredibly slow cast rate. 
 
 The significant Strength bonus granted by Enigma also allows characters to wear heavier gear without investing lots of Stat Points in Strength, such as a Spirit 
 
 Monarch . 
 
 However, Enigma does not boost any resistances, a pitfall that must be mitigated in higher difficulties. 
 
 Lastly, Enigma also comes with a high magic find modifier based on character level, making it a popular all-rounder runeword that most players will want at some stage for almost any character. 
 
 The most popular bases for Enigma are 
 
 Dusk Shroud , 
 
 Mage Plate and 
 
 Archon Plate .

title:
Enigma 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Runeword
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Enigma grants the 
 
 Teleport skill to any character regardless of their class, making it a valuable runeword that is useful for many builds. Notably, it isn't used often by Amazons due to their incredibly slow cast rate. 
 
 The significant Strength bonus granted by Enigma also allows characters to wear heavier gear without investing lots of Stat Points in Strength, such as a Spirit 
 
 Monarch . 
 
 However, Enigma does not boost any resistances, a pitfall that must be mitigated in higher difficulties. 
 
 Lastly, Enigma also comes with a high magic find modifier based on character level, making it a popular all-rounder runeword that most players will want at some stage for almost any character. 
 
 The most popular bases for Enigma are 
 
 Dusk Shroud , 
 
 Mage Plate and 
 
 Archon Plate .

title:
Enigma 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Runeword
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
When it comes to choosing the correct base for Enigma , everything quickly comes down to either 
 
 Mage Plate (MP), 
 
 Dusk Shroud (DS) or 
 
 Archon Plate (AP). While both DS and AP provide more Defense (like any Elite Armor does), MP has much lower STR requirements (only 55) compared to DS (77 STR) and AP (103 STR). So the question you have to answer yourself considering the "correct" base is if you value having a bit more defense (roughly around 200 - 250) over having more stat points to spent into Vitality. Furthermore, because of the low STR requirements of MP, its ideal to keep it in a shared stash and let multiple of your chars make use of it, MP is by far the most versatile and flexible base you could choose for Enigma while keeping a decent amount of Defense at the same time.

3

 Description

Silence was one of the most powerful runewords when runewords were first introduced, but has since been overshadowed by most other options. It was a popular option for Bowazons, due to its reasonable damage and high resists, but most characters use other gear in the latest versions of Diablo.

Vex runes are generally better used elsewhere, such as for Heart of the Oak or Exile .

title:
Silence 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Runeword
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Silence as a RW weapon for bows/xbows is an unorthodox yet fun alternative for a bowzon in my view. Not aimed at any build in particular, it can work for a phys dmg bowzon, but lacks the +skills for a frostmaiden or skills based Zon build. The most impressive stats on this RW is not the damage or combat stats, but rather the 75 res all and the 30% MF. The 200% ED is adequate, but definitely not endgame. Another unusual quirk of this weapon is the chance to cause monsters to flee. Thus, another possible use of this weapon is to equip it to the A1 rogue merc, instantly giving her high res all, MF, IAS, and hit causes flee. Overall, in a missile weapon, this RW is greatly overshadowed by 
 
 Windforce , Faith , and 
 
 Eaglehorn but is still a fun and unpopular alternative for bow builds. The stats on this weapon will never vary or roll, so if you got a spare 
 
 Vex lying around, then go for it if you want a strange but effective RW bow. The best RW bases for this weapon are 6 socketed 
 
 Hydra Bow and 
 
 Crusader Bow , in order to take advantage of the -20 req from the 
 
 Hel rune and high base damage of the RW base weapon.

4

7

title:
Silence 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Runeword
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
As a bit of trivia, Silence has the highest Hit Blinds Target modifier of any item by a significant margin. To put into perspective how profoundly large the chance to blind is, a ranged attacker would need to be 79 levels lower than the defender for there to be any chance whatsoever of a successful attack not blinding the target. For melee weapons, there would need to be a level difference of 119 to provide any chance of failure.

6

7

Alexandre_Souza

1 year ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post3773743.html?sid=7f9f9629d030a94cf7f91a3b6bfd2dbd#p3773743 
 
 1 year ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post3773743.html?sid=7f9f9629d030a94cf7f91a3b6bfd2dbd#p3773743

Not a bad weapon but exists much better options. For a melee weapon, Death and Breath of the Dying , which use a 
 
 Vex rune, grant much more damage. For a 
 
 Caster Weapon , a Heart of the Oak , which also uses a 
 
 Vex rune, grants +3 all Skils and 40% FCR, is a better option. Summary, a Silence weapon does much and nothing at the same time.

title:
Leaf 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Runeword
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
2 socket Staves 
 
 Adds 5-30 Fire Damage 
 
 +3 To Fire Skills 
 
 +3 To Fire Bolt (Sorceress Only) 
 
 +3 To Inferno (Sorceress Only) 
 
 +3 To Warmth (Sorceress Only) 
 
 +2 To Mana After Each Kill 
 
 + (2 Per Character Level) + 2-198 To Defense (Based On Character Level) 
 
 Cold Resist +33%

2 
 Description

Leaf is easily obtainable and one of the best weapons for a newly created Sorceress.

Tir and 
 
 Ral Runes can be farmed by Normal Countess within an average of 4-5 runs. The staff base can be bought from vendors in Act 1 and Act 2. 
 
 The mana after each kill is very helpful, as well as the boosts to fire skills and especially 
 
 Warmth . 
 
 Since one cannot wear a shield with this runeword, switching to a Spirit sword with Ancient's Pledge or Rhyme Shield in the second weapon slot can be a viable alternative once arriving in Nightmare difficulty.

Note that the "+3 To Fire Skills" modifier applies to all classes, making it a good early game weapon for not only Sorceress but Druid, Assassin and Warlock as well.

14

4 years ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post14898.html?sid=b3546e25c129a9ab48555f58eb61fb81#p14898 
 
 4 years ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post14898.html?sid=b3546e25c129a9ab48555f58eb61fb81#p14898

For sorceress, try to find a 2 socket staff at 
 
 Drognan (Act 2 normal) that has +1-3 to the specific skill(s) you want. For example, as a 
 
 Fireball sorceress I found a +3 
 
 Fireball 2 socket staff at 
 
 Drognan at level 19, giving me a total of +6 
 
 Fireball at level 19!

text:

 4 years ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post15392.html?sid=b3546e25c129a9ab48555f58eb61fb81#p15392

Assassin's that use this do not benefit from "+2 mana after each kill". (Or from any item that grants the life permutations of this as well) This is due to the fact that their traps do not count as the assassin killing the monsters, but instead as a minion doing it. 
 
 However, considering that the trap bug was fixed in D2R, this may mean that the underlying systems for the way traps operate and are perceived by the game world may have changed. And as a result, this mechanic may have also been fixed.

text:
Past in LoD 1.09, and before the 1.10 runewords, Fury was considered one of the best weapon for a barbarian ; especially in BvB when paired with 
 
 Stormshield . 
 Commonly made in a 
 
 Balrog Blade , it was a strong sword. 
 
 Only a magic weapon with the infamous magic affixes Cruel (201-300% ed) of Quickness (40% IAS) could compete. 
 
 It was also the most expensive runeword in the game and the only one with a rune higher than 
 
 Vex .

6

title:
Holy Thunder 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Runeword
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
4 socket Scepters 
 
 +60% Enhanced Damage 
 
 -25% Target Defense 
 
 Adds 5-30 Fire Damage 
 
 Adds 21-110 Lightning Damage 
 
 +75 Poison Damage Over 5 Seconds 
 
 +10 To Maximum Damage 
 
 Lightning Resistance +60% 
 
 +5 To Maximum Lightning Resistance 
 
 +3 To Holy Shock (Paladin Only) 
 
 Level 7 Chain Lightning (60 Charges)

2 
 Description

Holy Thunder is a reasonably powerful low-level melee runeword with a very modest barrier to entry. Because the player can obtain most of the necessary runes from the 
 
 Rescue on Mount Arreat quest reward, a 4os 
 
 War Scepter or 
 
 Divine Scepter is the most challenging acquisition needed to utilize the runeword. 
 
 A 
 
 War Scepter Holy Thunder will have a base damage value of 16-37, while a 
 
 Divine Scepter will have a base damage of 25-70 (assuming neither is of Superior quality). Note that the latter will have a clvl requirement of 25 rather than 21.

4 years ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post1299.html?sid=3e29b04cb13c136000a6cf174ef3310d#p1299 
 
 4 years ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post1299.html?sid=3e29b04cb13c136000a6cf174ef3310d#p1299

Honor is an early game runeword that provides some useful attributes, such as life leech, deadly strike and strength . 
 It's often used as a low level duel weapon. 
 
 The Runes can easily be farmed by Normal/Nightmare Countess.

3

7

Aelanwyr

1 year ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post3892349.html?sid=3e29b04cb13c136000a6cf174ef3310d#p3892349 
 
 1 year ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post3892349.html?sid=3e29b04cb13c136000a6cf174ef3310d#p3892349

Honor is a more well-rounded, damage-focused alternative to Strength and Spirit , though finding a 5os weapon may prove more challenging than the relatively accessible bases that can accommodate those other runewords. Once 5os bases are widely available, the runes required for runewords such as Black , Hustle , Obedience , Unbending Will , or Passion would also be more widely available; nevertheless, Honor is an excellent choice for boosting a low-level melee alt's clear speed.

However, three-socket weapons are available at much lower ilvls than five-socket weapons, making King's Grace a more accessible on-level option for characters that are using only items that they have found themselves.

title:
Lionheart 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Runeword
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Lionheart is quite possibly the *best* budget physical damage build's armor that one can get. Cheap and easy to make, the runes needed to make it are easily found in nightmare and hell. It could be considered a "Poor Man's Fortitude " in this way. In addition, this armor could also be a respectable hireling armor, as it boasts 30 all res, vitality, life bonus, ED%, as well as strength bonus, which will also increase the Act 2 mercenary's damage. Lastly, this armor has a -15% requirement, which will help lower the necessary strength requirement when placing it into heavier or higher level armors.

13

 Description

An excellent early game melee runeword for classes that struggle with damage in the early game (commonly Barbarian). 
 
 It is very easily farmable at 
 
 The Countess on Normal difficulty. 
 Finding an 
 
 El Rune takes an average of 10 Countess Runs, so keep your 
 
 El runes if you find one and plan to make this runeword!

 Perfect Skull in a Helmet or Armor.

 Halberd is formidable, while waiting for Insight 12 levels later (for example).

text:
3 socket Missile Weapons

 Knockback

Data contributed by Anonymous

2 
 Description

Melody might be one of the best weapons for a Hybrid Javazon or even a Bowazon, especially if the price needs to be taken into account. 
 
 Melody may only add little ED, but the additional IAS and+Skills are what makes this runeword stand out. 
 
 When combined with an Amazon-specific bow, this runeword can provide up to +6 Skills onto the Bow & Crossbow Skill tree. 
 
 Icemaidens and Hybrid builds can make good use of this runeword.

title:
Melody 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Runeword
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
This bow is an incredibly cheap and affordable RW and is an amazing choice for a skills based Amazon bow build, such as frostmaidens or fire/exploding/ 
 
 Immolation Arrow builds. In order to maximize this RW's effectiveness, build it into a +3 Amazon bow such as a 
 
 Matriarchal Bow or 
 
 Grand Matron Bow . If you have a +3 bow skills bow with 3 sockets sitting around, like if you did the socket recipe on that bow trying to get 4 sockets but it rolled 3, don't despair, use that 3 socket bow for this instead. This RW has no variance in it's stats when rolled, and the only downside is very low ED%, but as a skills based bowzon, you won't be relying on that to do damage.

2

7

Alexandre_Souza

1 year ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post3755456.html?sid=b9ed7be8e8504f41ad16055308819ac8#p3755456 
 
 1 year ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post3755456.html?sid=b9ed7be8e8504f41ad16055308819ac8#p3755456

Excellent rune word for leveling and initial end game bow for an elemental bowzon of frost or hybrid with 
 
 Exploding Arrow . It's very powerful when made in an Amazon bow (not necessary at elite bows) with +3 to Bow and Crossbow Skills .

2

Data contributed by Anonymous

2 
 Description

Memory is a mid game staff rune word. 
 
 Due to requiring a two handed weapon, is it not used as a main gear. It is used as a pre-buff item on switch instead. 
 For that reason, it is often made in staves with +3 
 
 Energy Shield bonus (granting a total of +9). Bonuses to other buff skills are a nice addition. 
 
 A similar item, also with +6 ES bonus is 
 
 Warpspear .

text:
4 years ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post975615.html?sid=d0cefab490533bc73584292626e4e261#p975615 
 
 4 years ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post975615.html?sid=d0cefab490533bc73584292626e4e261#p975615

Corrections: 
 
 (Thanks @jbiserkov for pointing my error) 
 
 So, out of curiosity, how soon can you get a 3 socket 
 
 Helm ? 
 
 Max Socket numbers are determined by iLvl (item level), which is usually mLvl (the level of the monster that dropped it). 
 
 Each base item has a different value of sockets for iLvl 1-25, then iLvl 26-40 and finally iLvl 41+. 
 
 When looking at the normal helms, for the non-class specific ones, the helms that can show 3 sockets are the ones bellow, which start to drop with 3 sockets at iLvl41+ :

Crown

Grand Crown

Great Helm

Mask 
 The class specific ones can start to drop with 3 sockets at iLvl 26+ :

Avenger Guard (Barbarian only)

Assault Helmet (Barbarian only)

Horned Helm (Barbarian only)

Fanged Helm (Barbarian only)

Jawbone Cap (Barbarian only)

Hawk Helm (Druid only)

Wolf Head (Druid only)

Antlers (Druid only)

Falcon Mask (Druid only)

title:
Radiance 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Runeword
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Spirit Mask (Druid only) 
 Considering Uniques mLvl are aLvl+3 (Area Level + 3) the soonest you can drop a non-class specific 3 socket 
 
 Helm is at areas with aLvl 38 (only for unique and their minions kill). 
 At aLvl 39 you can drop from champions and at aLvl 41 regular mosnter can drop one. 
 
 Looking at normal difficulty, I couldn't find any area with aLvl 38 but the first area with aLvl 39 is 
 
 Abaddon , which is not part of the game's quest progressions. 
 
 The first area that you'll visit during progression that has aLvl39 is 
 
 Worldstone Keep Level 1 . Also, 
 
 Worldstone Keep Level 3 has aLvl 42 which means normal monsters are able to drop a 
 
 Helm like this. 
 
 Also, some mosnter have a fixed mLvl. For example, Diablo has a mLvl of 40 in normal difficulty so not an option.

Nihlathak has a whopping mLvl of 68 on normal!

Thresh Socket has mLvl of exactly 41 (and "Socket" on his name )

title:
Radiance 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Runeword
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Frozenstein an mLvl of 46. 
 Not that you should be farming those super uniques on normal for 3os helms, as WSK lvl 3 would be way more efficient. 
 
 For the class specific itens (3os starting with iLvl 26), the first Lvl 23 areas are in Act 3. 
 Also the very first area of Act 4 normal ( 
 
 Outer Steppes ) is already aLvl 26 so would be your best bet for finding your base. 
 
 Besides Lore , another alternatives are the new in 2.6 runewords Ground , Cure and Bulwark . Those runeworks tipically outclass Radiance . 
 
 Original post bellow: 
 Spoiler

This is an okay runeword for it's price but by the time you find a 3 open socket 
 
 Helm (which is in Hell difficulty) you will probably have found something better. 
 
 Still might be useful when twinking a character but overall it is outclassed by Lore .

3

7

title:

text:
@crocorinho You don't need to be in Hell to find a 3os 
 
 Helm . 
 
 I got a 3os 
 
 Crown in the Normal difficulty Throne room (non-terrorized) (aLvl 43), I was very surprised. Normal The 
 
 Worldstone Keep Level 3 is aLvl 42, so that works too. 
 
 You can get a 3 open socket Crown , Mask or Great Helm , as long as item level is 41+ 
 
 Nightmare 
 
 Jail Level 1 is alvl 41, so from there on out you can find a 3os 
 
 Helm . 
 
 The same is true for the class-specific Barbarian/Druid only helms/pelts. 
 
 You are probably thinking of 3os Tiara s, which have a qLvl of 70, which means Hell 
 
 Monastery Gate onwards (except some caves, see https://diablo2.diablowiki.net/Area_Level )

title:
Rhyme 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Runeword
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Rhyme can often times be overlooked when the eye on the prize is 4 os 
 
 Monarch for your Spirt. However; Rhyme can be made as early as Act 2, equipped at 29 with a 25 Strength requirement, you can shop 
 
 Drognan for a 2 os 
 
 Bone Shield (which IMO looks the most bad ass out of all the vendor shields), and the runes are relatively easy to get (I mean if you're not farming Countess , are you really playing D2R ?). 
 
 This really is a shield any non-dual wield, wild cowboy 
 
 Frenzy Barbarian transitions into until you get your Ancient's Pledge or Spirit 
 
 Monarch or whatever you're gonna use through Hell difficulty, you could even transition into Splendor if you are struggling with a 
 
 Monarch base or Runes. Moreover, easy to throw this on a swap for 
 
 Duriel , or anything in Act 5 really with the " Cannot Be Frozen ". Realistically the baby brother to Spirit for a Sorceress with the +FCR and +Skills, +Mana Regen and, well really the +everything it has . If you're a new player, or just starting a new Class, nice to have a spare in your stash to move things along early game. Solid shield for Normal and even Nightmare difficulty.

1

7

2 
 Description

Venom , with its puzzling lack of Enhanced Damage, is one of the worst uses of a 
 
 Mal Rune in the game. By level 49, the 273 poison damage offered by this weapon is completely insignificant. The attributes of this weapon are ostensibly synergistic with 
 
 Poison Dagger , but even that use case seems ridiculous when compared to the benefits offered by Plague .

text:
Shako 
 
 Defense: 98-141 
 Durability: 12

 Req level: 62 
 Quality level: 69 
 Treasure class: 60 
 +2 To All Skills 
 
 + (1.5 Per Character Level) 1-148 To Life (Based On Character Level) 
 
 + (1.5 Per Character Level) 1-148 To Mana (Based On Character Level) 
 
 Damage Reduced By 10% 
 
 50% Better Chance of Getting Magic Items 
 
 +2 To All Attributes 
 
 Patch 1.09

Data contributed by Khegan

2 
 Description

Harlequin Crest - referred to within the community by its base item " 
 
 Shako " - is one of the best helms in the game for any character or build, granting a large bonus to life, mana, and skills. The +2 to All Attributes may seem odd, but this is based off of the Diablo 1 unique by the same name, which has the same effect. 
 
 In 1.08, 
 
 Harlequin Crest had Indestructible, 100% Enhanced Defense, 40% to Max Life, and 50% Magic Find. With 
 
 Arkaine's Valor and a few other items, the 1.08 version is highly sought after on battle.net nonladder.

Description by Aelanwyr

 4 years ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post2143604.html?sid=52065ba179c99a9755c1fd2a5332f36c#p2143604

Harlequin Crest is the best Magic Find helm in the game. 
 
 Besides providing a big amount of MF it also has the very sought after +2 to All Skills on the helmet slot, lots of life and mana and the rare Damage Reduced By X% property! 
 
 It is very commom to add a socket to a 
 
 Harlequin Crest (with 
 
 Larzuk ) and fill it with 1x 
 
 Perfect Topaz or, if rich enough, 1x 
 
 Ist Rune. 
 
 Since it have a base 50% MF it will end up with a Magic Find chance comparable to a 3 open sockets helmet filled with the same jewel/rune yet with lots of other awesome magic properties.

title:
Hellfire Torch 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Unique Item
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
When farming for your desired torch (or to get rich in ladder quickly) NEVER identify a 
 
 Hellfire Torch when it drops, instead sell the unidentified torch for runes. 
 
 You can then use these freshly aquired runes to buy a new 
 
 3x3 Keyset , for farming a new 
 
 Hellfire Torch . 
 
 The process is quick and straight forward (typically 30 minutes including buying keys and selling torches), requires a rune worth one 
 
 3x3 Keyset and a basic smiter paladin with cheap gear (for fun and a well written guide google poor man smiter); and allows you to build the Wealth for buying the torch you desire. 
 
 Typically you turn one rune invested for the 
 
 3x3 Keyset into four runes for the unid 
 
 Hellfire Torch - buying keysets is faster than farming them yourself. 
 
 Why does it work? Most torches you find and identify are worthless (the famous Druid torch 10/10 comes to my mind) - typically prices are also worse than the 
 
 Key sets you bought for acquiring the torch. See the comment from Gozdev above for your chance of finding your desired torch.

Seraph's Hymn 
 
 Normal Unique 
 Amulet 
 
 Req level: 65 
 Quality level: 73 
 +2 To All Skills 
 
 + 1-2 To Defensive Auras (Paladin Only) 
 
 + 25-50 % Damage To Demons 
 
 + 150-250 To Attack Rating Against Demons 
 
 + 25-50 % Damage To Undead 
 
 + 150-250 To Attack Rating Against Undead 
 
 +2 To Light Radius 
 
 Patch 1.10

Data contributed by Khegan

Seraph's Hymn , while very rare, is outclassed by more popular amulets, such as 
 
 Mara's Kaleidoscope . Most Paladin builds care little for skill bonuses to Defensive Auras, and the damage/attack rating increase vs. demons and undead is not enough to warrant its use over other options.

title:
Alma Negra 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Unique Item
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Sacred Rondache 
 
 Defense: 461-511 
 Durability: 68 
 Req Strength: 109 
 Req level: 77 
 Quality level: 85 
 Treasure class: 72 
 Base block: 78% 
 Paladin smite dmg: 35-58 
 + 180-210 % Enhanced Defense 
 
 + 1-2 To Paladin Skill Levels 
 
 20% Increased Chance of Blocking 
 
 30% Faster Block Rate 
 
 + 40-75 % Enhanced Damage 
 
 40-75 % Bonus to Attack Rating 
 
 Magic Damage Reduced By 5-9 
 
 Patch 1.10

Data contributed by Stormlash

2 
 Description

Alma Negra is an offensive-focused shield that has the rare +% Enhanced Damage modifier in the shield-slot. In that regard it is second only to the Phoenix runeword which is very expensive but boasts a massive +350-400% Enhanced Damage modifier. Other than that, the bonus attack rating and +skills can also give an DPS edge to a paladin. 
 
 The +% Enhanced Damage modifier on the shield is applied after all the +% Enhanced Damage modifier of the weapon itself but before any skill damage calculation so, for example, a +70% Enchanced Damage mod on 
 
 Alma Negra would NOT mean a total +70% DPS boost to your final damage.

 Description

Andariel's Visage is frequently considered to be the best option for a mercenary, particularly if ethereal. 
 
 It is also among the best options for many physical builds, on par with 
 
 Guillaume's Face , and generally surpassing 
 
 Vampire Gaze if you have another source of Mana Steal. 
 
 It will often be socketed with a 15% IAS, 30% Fire Resist jewel to offset the -30 Fire Resist penalty.

Andariel's Visage (Elite Unique 
 
 Demonhead ) price range in Diablo 2: Resurrected (as of July 2025): 
 
 Typical rolls (non-ethereal): Most 
 
 Andariel's Visage helms trade for 
 
 Um to 
 
 Ist rune. Lower rolls (e.g., <27 Strength, <9% Life Leech) may go for 
 
 Um , while good rolls (e.g., 27+ Strength, 9–10% Life Leech) are closer to 
 
 Ist or slightly higher. 
 
 High rolls: Nearly perfect stats (30 Strength, 10% Life Leech, 150% Enhanced Defense) can fetch 2–3 
 
 Ist runes or a combination of 
 
 Ist +

 Perfect Gems . 
 
 Ethereal version: Ethereal 
 
 Andariel's Visage is much rarer and can reach 
 
 Vex rune or higher, especially for perfect rolls. 
 
 Low demand: Trade volume is moderate, but prices are stable due to its popularity for mercenaries and melee builds.

Be careful when seeing the 
 
 Diablo Clone Messages popping up on your screen. DClone will replace the closest Superunique to your location (when outside of an Act town). Meaning, if you are in Chaos Santuary trying to kill Diablo, the clone can replace one of the seal bosses. The Mini Uber bosses ( 
 
 Lilith , 
 
 Uber Duriel , and Uber Izual ) are also Superuniques. So you might be out a set of keys if Dclone spawns while running Ubers since the organ piece will not drop if Dclone has replaced one of them.

title:
Arachnid Mesh 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Unique Item
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
An early (for the Reign of the Warlock's cycle) observation is that 
 
 Gheed's Wager is now another unique 
 
 Belt that can reach up to 20% FCR. It also has a smattering of other benefits like All Resistances, FHR, FRW, and -Enemy Magic Resistance, while +1 All Skills is missing. 
 
 The sheer number of variables in a 
 
 Gheed's Wager is an obstacle and it doesn't seem to drop often enough so far after 2 weeks of intense play, so getting a max 20% FCR one might be a challenge. Not that 
 
 Arachnid Mesh drops left and right either... 
 
 At first glance, I preferred 
 
 Arachnid Mesh 's +1 All Skills over 
 
 Gheed's Wager 's other benefits, but having All Resistances on a 
 
 Belt feels rather attractive sometimes. 
 
 Something to consider when you start thinking about reaching ~20% FCR with your 
 
 Belt slot.

3

title:
Arioc's Needle 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Unique Item
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Hyperion Spear 
 
 2H damage: 98-392 
 Base speed: -10 
 Durability: 30 
 Req Strength: 155 
 Req Dexterity: 120 
 Req level: 81 
 Quality level: 85 
 Treasure class: 60 
 + 180-230 % Enhanced Damage 
 
 50% Deadly Strike 
 
 +394 Poison Damage Over 10 Seconds 
 
 +30% Increased Attack Speed 
 
 + 2-4 To All Skills 
 
 Ignore Target's Defense 
 
 Patch 1.10

Data contributed by Dzik

2 
 Description

For much of the game's history, 
 
 Arioc's Needle was a key without a lock, if one would pardon the pun. It faced stiff competition from a plethora of potent runewords for Desert Mercenary followers, was too unfocused for the already subpar Spear -Zon, lagged behind other two-handers for Barbarians, required one too many hands to be of interest to Paladins or Assassins, had an unfavorable IAS chart for Druids, and was the direct inferior of multiple staves for casting-focused Necromancers and Sorceresses. 
 
 It was not until the introduction of the Warlock, who can use this 
 
 Hyperion Spear in "one hand" – and whose 
 
 Echoing Strike skill covets the unusual intersection of +4 to All Skills and 50% Deadly Strike – that this endgame weapon finally found its niche.

text:
With Warlock being in the roster, 
 
 Arioc's Needle is a interesting weapon choice for hybrid Demon Skill Tree and Eldrich Skill Tree Warlock. 
 
 The +4 Skills from 
 
 Arioc's Needle help immensely and only 1 skill away from a +3 auto mod on Daggers for Void . Also it is much easier to get your hands on 
 
 Arioc's Needle than a 
 
 Zod rune. 
 
 In conjunction with 
 
 Levitation Mastery , the Deadly Strike on 
 
 Arioc's Needle furthers your crit chance and you can use 
 
 Arioc's Needle with 40% less requirements if your 
 
 Levitation Mastery is at level 20. Very solid piece of equipment on Warlock.

7

2 
 Description

Arkaine's Valor gives a high bonus to life and skills, making it one of the better unique body armors. However, it still falls behind compared to runewords such as Chains of Honor , Enigma , and Fortitude . 
 
 In 1.08, it had 200% Enhanced Defense, 2.5 Vitality per level, 30% Faster Hit Recovery, +2 Life per level, +2 to All Skills, Indestructible, and 50% Fire Resist. The absolutely insane bonuses to life made it one of the most powerful items in the game at the time, and a few copies with many more duped copies are available on non-ladder for hefty prices.

title:
Arkaine's Valor 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Unique Item
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Arkaine's Valor gives a high bonus to life and skills, making it one of the better unique body armors. However, it still falls behind compared to runewords such as Chains of Honor , Enigma , and Fortitude . 
 
 In 1.08, it had 200% Enhanced Defense, 2.5 Vitality per level, 30% Faster Hit Recovery, +2 Life per level, +2 to All Skills, Indestructible, and 50% Fire Resist. The absolutely insane bonuses to life made it one of the most powerful items in the game at the time, and a few copies with many more duped copies are available on non-ladder for hefty prices.

5

7

ayrie

4 years ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post1626695.html?sid=fe48ed46d7dfdf3663b4db24528b407f#p1626695 
 
 4 years ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post1626695.html?sid=fe48ed46d7dfdf3663b4db24528b407f#p1626695

Those who've played Diablo 1 likely obtained this armor through a quest discovered about a third of the way through Diablo's labyrinth. 
 
 Arkaine's Valor was by far the best armor the player could obtain up to that point, and could in fact be used by Rogues and Sorcerers until the end of the game.

19

 Description

title:
Arm of King Leoric 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Unique Item
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
As far as skill bonuses go, Arm of King Leoric is certainly one of the best wands for a Necromancer focused on summoning. In fact, it's very difficult to find a magic wand that even comes close to what's offered. 
 
 The only downside is the chance to cast Bone Prison when hit: this could trap a character with a dangerous opponent in melee range. Having an item with Teleport charges (or, more costly, Enigma ) is recommended to avoid this scenario. 
 
 Commonly paired with Beast : summon an army with this wand equipped, and then switch to Beast to provide a powerful offensive aura. Minions summoned in excess of lost skill points will expire, but those that remain will retain their increased combat stats.

Description by Khegan

title:
Astreon's Iron Ward 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Unique Item
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Astreon's Iron Ward is a great item for Zealot Paladins for the + to Combat Skills, Crushing Blow and IAS. The attack rating bonus and damage reduction are also useful.

10

7

Nate

3 years ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post3240758.html?sid=a9dddc505b321073d42ab0f2875e03af#p3240758 
 
 3 years ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post3240758.html?sid=a9dddc505b321073d42ab0f2875e03af#p3240758

I was happy to find this in the pits, it’s super rare. And despite what people say, it’s a damn good weapon for an Uber smiter, I added a sheal to it and it brought down the Ubers with ease. A good option if you don’t have a 
 
 Lo for a Grief . 
 
 It is probably a good idea to repair it after the mini ubers, it’s got good durability, but no-one wants to head back to town right in the middle of fighting 
 
 Mephisto to repair a weapon. 
 
 With the +4 to combat skills, it’s probably an option for basically any pally build

14

7

YugoHell

I like using an ethereal version of it on my 
 
 Zeal Paladin. And it is actually fantastic since it has all the required modifiers like flat damage, crushing blow, increased attack speed, + combat skills. The only problem is one needs absolutely an ethereal one to create enough damage output, and it requires 
 
 Zod rune of course.

 Description

The greatest of these heroes was 
 
 Izual , lieutenant to the Arch-Angel 
 
 Tyrael and bearer of the Angelic Runeblade 
 
 Azurewrath .

Azurewrath is powerful against enemies in Hell difficulty because of its huge damage bonuses, especially the added magic damage, as there are few monsters immune to magic. 
 
 In addition to its added damage stats, it boasts a helpful +1 to All Skills, and is strong against undead mobs thanks to the 
 
 Sanctuary Aura it grants when equipped (although the knockback from the aura is somehow annoying in melee combat).

title:
Bane Ash 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Unique Item
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Staves cannot be obtained through gambling , which means that if you want a 
 
 Bane Ash , you will have to farm for it. 
 
 It is a slightly rare TC3 item. If you are working on your Holy Grail or Chronicle and are missing some low level unique items, my recommendation is to target-farm this item before attempting to gamble for some other low level uniques, as it is highly likely that you will find quite a few of those other items along the way. 
 
 I initially used my 
 
 Holy Fire Auradin to sweep farm Normal Act 1 for several hours, but had no luck. I then switched to my Barb with 771mf and 65% 
 
 Find Item , and target-farmed 
 
 Coldcrow in Normal. 
 
 Bane Ash finally dropped through 
 
 Find Item on the 167th run. During the whole process, I found 8x 
 
 Pelta Lunata (including one perfect, and one ethereal), 4x 
 
 The Hand of Broc , 2x 
 
 Lenymo , 2x 
 
 Rixot's Keen , 3x 
 
 Biggin's Bonnet , 3x 
 
 Hotspur , 1x 
 
 Serpent Lord , 1x 
 
 Gull , 1x 
 
 Greyform . A total of more than 20 Short Staves dropped but rolled to be magic or rare.

1

 Description

Bing Sz Wang can be seen as the older sibling of 
 
 Shadowfang and the younger sibling of 
 
 Azurewrath , providing a considerable amount of physical and especially cold damage given its level, with the added bonus of a 5% chance of casting 
 
 Frozen Orb on strike. 
 
 It deals a significant amount of damage for its level requirement. The fact that it also includes a -30% to requirements stat makes it a weapon that can be easily used early on by a variety of characters and builds, while most high level unique weapons cannot.

title:
Bing Sz Wang 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Unique Item
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Bing Sz Wang is a similar and more powerful weapon to the 
 
 Shadowfang unique 
 
 Two-handed Sword (somewhat like the 
 
 Hexfire vs 
 
 Hellplague unique swords), providing higher damage output & lower damage loss while single-hand wielding, but without the life & mana leech that 
 
 Shadowfang provides. It has half the chill duration as 
 
 Shadowfang , but adds 2 to Freeze Target and provides much more cold damage. Both swords help with preventing monsters from being revived, as they shatter and leave no corpse when killed while chilled (20% chance) or frozen (100% chance). 
 
 Note that monster chill & freeze times are 1/2 as long in Nightmare difficulty, and 1/4 as long in Hell difficulty. Also, if the total chill time from all gear is longer than the result of the Freeze Target calculation, monsters will stay chilled after unfreezing. 
 
 An interesting build could involve a Barbarian dual-wielding these after them being upgraded and socketed with a cold facet, though cold immune monsters would definitely be an issue due to the fairly low physical damage output and lack of any other elemental damage. This also means the weapon isn't very viable late into the game.

5

2 
 Description

Blackbog's Sharp is a weapon designed for poison necromancers. As a dagger , it allows using 
 
 Poison Dagger skill, however it is rather unpopular. 
 
 High skill bonuses make 
 
 Blackbog's Sharp a viable midgame alternative for wands, until we find a decent base for White . High dex requirement limits its usability, though, unless you plan to use max block. 
 
 Unlike most wands, it lacks FCR, sought after by casters, but this is not an major issue for poison necromancer, since poison damage is applied over time and subsequent casts of 
 
 Poison Nova only refresh its duration.

title:
Blackhorn's Face 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Unique Item
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
This is item is incredibly powerfull against uber diablo if you play amazon. 
 1. it shuts down his high regeneration 
 2. it slows down his 
 
 Charge attempts making them easily avoidable 
 3. and the best: it virtually nullifies the threat from his red lightning hose attack, if you don't want to encounter him so close that the attack goes over your head. 
 His attack is a series of many hits, which don't pose a threat from a single hit, but from the sheer number of hits. The lightning absorption which is applied on each single hit can nearly cancel out the damage with only a mediocre amount of lightning resist required and turns the fight with an amazon into a standing shootout. This works great because the absorption on the item is a fixed number and not a percentage as the amount of damage of a single hit is rather small.

15

7

title:
Blackoak Shield 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Unique Item
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Blackoak Shield is most useful for its ability to absorb large amounts of Cold damage. This allows the wearer to be nearly immune to Cold damage. 
 
 However, it only has Half Freeze Duration, so gear such as 
 
 Raven Frost must still be worn, and Cold damage is not a particularly common concern, as it tends to deal only small amounts of damage that can be further mitigated by 
 
 Thawing Potion . 
 
 The rest of its bonuses are very reasonable, but they may not be enough to use this shield over more powerful options, such as Spirit or 
 
 Stormshield .

text:
Scimitar 
 
 1H damage: 3-10 
 Base speed: -20

 Patch 1.09

Data contributed by Dzik

2 
 Description

This early weapon is excellent for any starting melee, offering an extremely high level of Life Steal, decent IAS (bearing in mind it is 
 
 Scimitar base weapon type, so -20 weapon speed to begin with) along with a substantial bonus to Life, all Resistances and if that package were not complete enough, a decent chance at Open Wounds as well. 
 
 A stunningly good 1H weapon for the anyone, but especially an CH player, wanting to use a shield and still wreck the opposition.

text:
Matriarchal Bow 
 
 2H damage: 56-155 
 Base speed: -10

 + 180-230 % Enhanced Damage

 Fires Explosive Arrows Or Bolts [Level 13] 
 
 Level 5 Revive (30 charges) 
 
 + 2-4 To Bow And Crossbow Skills (Amazon Only) 
 
 Patch 1.10

Data contributed by Stormlash

2 
 Description

Blood Raven's Charge is a strong option for Bowazons with good damage and a large boost to bow and crossbow skills. The 'Fires Explosive Arrows ' mod only works for normal attacks and not skills; it does, however, use the Explosive Arrow skill without paying for the mana cost. 
 
 It also grants the 
 
 Revive skill, which makes it possible for the Amazon to summon a small army to act as a meat shield.

4 years ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post2110384.html?sid=dc2da76878cbe8c0a960c52207f9accf#p2110384 
 
 4 years ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post2110384.html?sid=dc2da76878cbe8c0a960c52207f9accf#p2110384

These gloves are a great option early on for physical damage builds. With 10% IAS, life bonus, FHR, and +5 damage, it's one of the better normal uniques compared to others available. It's also highly likely to be much better than any rares encountered in normal and early NM. Some builds can still make use of these gloves in NM and Hell, but at that point it should be considered a 'budget' or less than ideal choice, as good rares, crafted 
 
 Blood Gloves , and better set or unique options are available. Lacking any of those, these are serviceable however.

 Patch 1.09

Data contributed by Dzik

2 
 Description

Bloodletter is a great progression weapon due to having great damage, attack speed and life leech for it's required character level of 30. 
 
 Barbarians dual-wielding those will feel a massive power spike at char lvl 30, allowing them to destroy everything in their path even on /players 8 difficulty for the entirety of normal. The swords will still be pretty decent through nightmare assuming the rest of his gear is also good (with the occasional /players x scaling down). 
 
 It's worth noting two of these can grant from +4 up to a whopping +8 to 
 
 Blade Mastery and +2 to +6 to 
 
 Whirlwind .

title:
Boneshade 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Unique Item
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Boneshade is one of the top weapon choices for a bonemancer, primarily thanks to its high skill bonuses to both, high and low level bone spells. Its most direct top-tier competitor would be White wand which in a perfect base could offer an extra +3 to 
 
 Bone Spear and +2 to 
 
 Bone Spirit respectively, coming at the cost of -4 to 
 
 Teeth , -1 to 
 
 Bone Armor and an available socket among others. 
 
 Additionally, the 25% faster cast rate on 
 
 Boneshade is typically just enough to reach the critical breakpoints in common top-tier necromancer gear combinations. 
 
 The main drawback of 
 
 Boneshade as compared to alternatives like Heart of the Oak is the complete lack of bonus mana and resistances.

         • diablo2.io
text:
The skill level (slvl) of 
 
 Holy Bolt cast when struck is not randomly selected when the 
 
 Boneslayer Blade is dropped - rather, it is calculated from the ilvl and the native required level (rlvl) of the skill, which is 6 in case of 
 
 Holy Bolt . The list below shows the relationship between the slvl of the 
 
 Holy Bolt CtC effect and ilvl. 
 
 ilvl 50 = slvl 12 
 
 ilvl 54 = slvl 13 
 
 ilvl 58 = slvl 14 
 
 ilvl 62 = slvl 15 
 
 ilvl 66 = slvl 16 
 
 ilvl 70 = slvl 17 
 
 ilvl 74 = slvl 18 
 
 ilvl 78 = slvl 19 
 
 ilvl 82 = slvl 20

0

 Description

Glorious in appearance, but not as effective as it looks, 
 
 Brainhew has some good bonuses that don't necessarily compliment each other. 
 
 On the damage scale, 
 
 Brainhew is actually behind in comparison to other unique axes. Physical fighters will probably find its added Mana leech more useful, allowing them more leeway in using their skills, but other than the boosts to mana, 
 
 Brainhew really has little else to flaunt.

title:
Buriza-Do Kyanon 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Unique Item
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Quite possibly the best xbow of them all in D2. Although xbows are always a step behind bows, they can still prove useful in certain builds and can hold their own. The Buriza is best suited for a Bowazon, since it's effects are amplified by using 
 
 Strafe and multi-shot, inflicting freeze status and piercing multiple enemies per shot. Although lacking in build diversity and possibilities for other classes like the 
 
 Demon Machine due to DM's exploding 
 
 Bolts , Buriza makes up for this with it's hitpower potential. With a scaling max dmg per level, the Buriza is the strongest DPS exceptional xbow, and even makes the elite xbows pale in comparison, out damaging them with physical dmg. The Buriza is a great secondary choice or budget option for a bowazon, if the build wants a swap weapon to slow enemies down with the freeze and hit several targets with 
 
 Pierce , and then swap back to the primary DPS bow.

9

7

text:
An upped Buriza can absolutely work as a budget endgame substitute for 
 
 Windforce , Faith , or Mist . It does have a high str requirement but negates the need to put skill points or dedicate gear to 
 
 Pierce . Additional gear should focus on boosting damage through %ED rather than IAS, as further breakpoints will grant only negligible benefit with this weapon.

text:
9 months ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post3975723.html?sid=f89d3b9a6c594945df40f66d2b040e6a#p3975723 
 
 9 months ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post3975723.html?sid=f89d3b9a6c594945df40f66d2b040e6a#p3975723

Fun fact: this is probably one of, if not THE most famous Diablo item. Reason being it was an endgame carry item in the original Dota ( Defense of the ancients) mod for Frozen Throne. I remember the tooltip was something like:”When they introduced it, it was good. But then in 1.0x they IMPROVED it...”. 
 I specifically remember the all caps, because I kept wondering what the tooltip meant until I learned later that it was named after a Diablo item. 
 
 Sadly, this piece of knowledge is almost lost to time because when Valve made Dota 2, they had to rename Buriza to Deadalus due to copyright.

title:
Carin Shard 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Unique Item
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
This seems to be a typo of "cairn" which is a pile of stones often used as a simple memorial. The base item being petrified further supports this. 
 
 It makes a good budget golem summoning wand since it gives a total of +3 to all skills in the summon tree and a golem's stats are fixed once it's summoned. Make your walking tank with the Shard then swap back to 
 
 Arm of King Leoric , White , etc.

6

 Twister spam is what helps the most. Zealot pal, Fury shifter, 
 
 Frenzy barb, 
 
 Strafe zon - all of them will enjoy this ring's twisters. Tried n tested.

This ring adds an additional layer of protection against enemies that 
 
 Charge in or are close. 
 
 A wall of randomly generated twisters on attack stop fatal enemies like Stygian Dolls in their tracks and can prevent enemies from using skills by briefly stunning them. 
 
 Works especially well on 
 
 Strafe Amazons in areas like Durance of Hate or Worldstone Keep.

title:
Cerebus' Bite 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Unique Item
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Blood Spirit 
 
 Defense: 335-350 
 Durability: 20 
 Req Strength: 86 
 Req level: 63 
 Quality level: 71 
 Treasure class: 63 
 + 130-140 % Enhanced Defense 
 
 60-120 % Bonus to Attack Rating 
 
 7-10 % Life Stolen Per Hit 
 
 33% Chance of Open Wounds 
 
 + 2-4 To Shape Shifting Skills (Druid Only) 
 
 + 1-2 To Feral Rage (Druid Only) 
 
 Patch 1.10

Data contributed by Stormlash

2 
 Description

Cerebus' Bite is one of four Unique Druid Pelts, bearing a more martial bent than the two all-rounders - 
 
 Jalal's Mane and 
 
 Spirit Keeper - and the caster-oriented 
 
 Ravenlore . With its high Chance of Open Wounds, it can be a helpful source of health regen suppression. Bonuses to Life Stolen per Hit and 
 
 Feral Rage can augment a 
 
 Werewolf 's durability, while increased Attack Rating can help land 
 
 Fury: Skill , 
 
 Maul: Skill , and 
 
 Fire Claws strikes. Overall, its lack of defensive attributes make it a less popular choice than its counterparts, while the runeword Metamorphosis has further encroached upon this Unique's niche.

Description by Aelanwyr

4

text:
Chain Gloves 
 
 Defense: 27-28 
 Durability: 16 
 Req Strength: 25 
 Req level: 15 
 Quality level: 20 
 Treasure class: 12 
 + 20-30 % Enhanced Defense 
 
 +15 Defense 
 
 +25 To Attack Rating 
 
 25-40 % Better Chance of Getting Magic Items 
 
 200% Extra Gold From Monsters 
 
 +2 To Light Radius 
 
 Patch 1.09

Data contributed by Dzik

2 
 Description

Standard gloves for any magic/ 
 
 Gold find character builds. Cheap and easy to find off of normal/nightmare difficulty bosses. 
 
 Any fresh sorceress just starting out will likely want to have a pair of these either equipped or stashed away for those late night 
 
 Mephisto runs! Pairs well with 
 
 Goldwrap , 
 
 Skullder's Ire , and 
 
 Harlequin Crest . 
 
 Most magic find characters shoot for at least 200 MF, while some try to get between 300-350. The most important thing is you don’t want to sacrifice too much killing speed, as magic find does have diminishing returns.

Data contributed by Dzik

2 
 Description

As its name suggests, 
 
 Chromatic Ire grants bonuses to all three elements for the Sorceress, and is best used by tri-elemental builds, since other unique items outclass it for purely one element. Besides its general +skills to the Sorceress and + to all elemental masteries, it grants good stats for any Sorceress or other casters with its big life boost, all resist and FCR.

 Description

Corpsemourn can be useful for attacking characters more than casters with its added damage and casting 
 
 Iron Maiden when struck. The armour also grants 
 
 Corpse Explosion , which is a strong spell even at low levels since its damage doesn't scale with levels (only its radius does). This provides the wearer with a ranged tool to deal with adversaries.

Currently using it on a Sorceress Build I started this week and honestly just blazed past Act 1-3 once I hit lvl 11 to wear the set. 
 
 If you're doing a fire build it will carry you in to Act 4 easily, allowing you to pivot to another build or gear set later on.

5

7

Mercer

4 years ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post2457853.html?sid=35d8e999ab151e4a44850db33eb46330#p2457853 
 
 4 years ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post2457853.html?sid=35d8e999ab151e4a44850db33eb46330#p2457853

I'd say forget everything except 
 
 Cathan's Visage and 
 
 Cathan's Rule . But these two give casters at lvl 11 +70 to Mana, mana regen and +15-30 fire damage whenever you run out of mana. And 70 mana is huge at this stage. There is no other item combination that I know of that gives more at this point. And ofc you get +1 fire skills if you need that. 
 And Cathan must have been one the greatest sorcerers of all time. You can even find this gear in hell so he must have been there with this equipment! Legend..dary!

Something I've recently found out: This set is lovely for leveling a 
 
 Holy Fire zealot early game. The strength, res, LSPH, mana, Fdamage, and AR boost makes it fit a young palli very well. And no need for FCR because you're just using the aura, 
 
 Charge , HS, and 
 
 Sacrifice as alt abilities. Run that with Angelic sword and ring and you've got an interesting and effective build.

text:
Battle Staff 
 
 2H damage: 6-13 
 Base speed: [0]

 Req level: 11

 Treasure class: 18 
 +1 to Fire Skills 
 
 +10 Maximum Fire Damage 
 
 +50% Damage To Undead 
 
 +50 Mana (2 set items) 
 All Resistances +10 (3 set items)

Part of set: Cathan's Traps

Data contributed by Khegan

2 
 Description

Cathan's Rule , with its focus on Fire Skills, is ostensibly designed for use by the Sorceress. With that said, it faces stiff competition from even Common-quality staves, which can sport useful staffmods that could provide a Sorceress with greater bonuses to skills at a lower level requirement. 
 
 Cathan's Rule has a limited window of usefulness due to the ease with which Leaf can be assembled. However, during levels 11-18, Cathan's Rule could be an effective choice for Fire Druids, 
 
 Wake of Fire Assassins, or 
 
 Holy Fire Paladins.

text:
5 years ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post87.html?sid=f40dbf1cddea4e62b12790ea95f78b9b#p87 
 
 5 years ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post87.html?sid=f40dbf1cddea4e62b12790ea95f78b9b#p87

Cathan's Seal is one of the best early game items for any physical figher. Although the damage reduction isn't much, 6% life steal is powerful even in Nightmare. Although the set isn't very good as a whole, the +10 to strength bonus on the rings won't go amiss on any character.

1

7

ayrie

4 years ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post1626314.html?sid=f40dbf1cddea4e62b12790ea95f78b9b#p1626314 
 
 4 years ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post1626314.html?sid=f40dbf1cddea4e62b12790ea95f78b9b#p1626314

There are only two set-quality rings in the game - this and 
 
 Angelic Halo - and both are worthless for trading purposes. 
 
 I can't be the only one who nonetheless instinctively picks up unidentified set ring drops anyway.

6

7

title:

         • diablo2.io
text:
2 
 Description

This amulet exists to tease a magic finding Sorceress by giving her the vainglorious hope of finding a 
 
 Tal Rasha's Adjudication , only to reveal itself as yet another worthless 
 
 Cathan's Sigil . 
 
 Really though, the two piece set bonus is decent for low level physical damage characters if worn with the ring, although such characters would likely be better off with the absurd attack power bonus provided by wearing 
 
 Angelic Halo and 
 
 Angelic Wings together.

text:
If you're planning on use 
 
 Find Item with your barbarian while using this weapon, rethink it - the added cold damage shatters a lot of bodies. If you're using the full set, that's even worse - the Freeze Target property plus the added cold damage ensure that 99% of the bodies will shatter. 
 
 And it has been told on the legends that Necromancers get really mad around barbarians wielding Immortal King 's.

17

7

Although it pales in comparison to late-game runewords, the "IK 
 
 Maul " has its charms. With a generous 40% Increased Attack Speed, >33% Crushing Blow, two open sockets, and an ever-increasing list of elemental damage tacked on as the set is completed, this weapon is more than sufficient to clear most content.

Data contributed by Khegan

2 
 Description

Every instance of the 
 
 Immortal King's Detail spawns with identical attributes. With the full set equipped, its Defense value increases to 247. 
 
 Upgrading the 
 
 Immortal King's Detail with 
 
 Recipe: Elite Version of Set Armor will never result in a reduction in Defense. Because the minimum Strength required to equip a 
 
 Colossus Girdle (185) is less than the 232 needed to equip the 
 
 Immortal King's Soul Cage minus the 25 Strength this item provides, there is little downside to doing so if one intends to equip the full set. 
 
 With the full Immortal King set equipped, a 
 
 Colossus Girdle version of this item will provide 263-283 Defense.

title:
Immortal King 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected 
                                  Full Set
                                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Prior to Patch 2.4, 
 
 Whirlwind only used the base speed of the weapon and any Increased Attack Speed that was found on the weapon itself. In the case of the an unsocketed 
 
 Immortal King's Stone Crusher , this meant that WW would "swing" every 6 frames. With an additional +30% IAS on the 
 
 Maul itself (through any combination of 
 
 Shael runes and Jewels of Fervor), the maximum breakpoint of 4-frames-per-swing could be reached. 
 
 Following Patch 2.4, the IAS breakpoints for 
 
 Whirlwind factor in off-weapon IAS, and the 4-frame breakpoint is no longer obtainable for two-handed weapons other than swords. While an unsocketed 
 
 Immortal King's Stone Crusher equipped alongside the 
 
 Immortal King's Forge will still reach the 6-frame breakpoint, an additional +60% Increased Attack Speed from other sources is needed to reach the 5-frame-per-attack breakpoint (the new maximum). 
 
 This change, combined with 2.4 subjecting 
 
 Whirlwind to the attack speed penalties of Chill and Slow effects, has dramatically marred what was arguably the set's preeminent use-case. To add insult to injury, 2.4's change in 
 
 Whirlwind 's +% Damage from [-58+8*slvl]% Damage to [+25+5*slvl]%

Data contributed by Stormlash

2 
 Description

Aldur's Watchtower is a moderately good set in theory, but the high level requirement of 76 on the body armor prevents this set from being of much use. The damage on 
 
 Aldur's Rhythm is fairly good for its level (42), but it's inadequate at best once you can wear the body armor. 
 
 The 350% Enhanced Damage is on the complete set, not on the weapon. Enhanced Damage from non-weapon equipment works like Enhanced Damage from skills and the strength bonus, which is different from how Enhanced Damage on the weapon works. Essentially, the 350% ED will add 350% of your weapon damage to your total. With a 60-93 damage weapon, that means the 350% Enhanced Damage will add 210-325 total damage. This is really not very significant, but it's not a bug, just a balance issue.

text:
Another interesting use, I once made an effective PvP/PvM Aldurs 
 
 Hurricane /Shock-Bear hybrid with 7 cold facets for +/- 35% cold damage, you hit around 3200 
 
 Hurricane damage and with 9 elemental/frw GCs you can really scoot I have some old vids back in the days on it on YT. One of the benefits Aldurs brings is a lot of stats, max resists, an open shield for 
 
 Stormshield , but most importantly those 7 open sockets just give a lot of flexibility in the build now the Attack Speed calculation is mostly calculated off 
 
 Maul charges (fanatcism/burst) and much less on weapon speed, no longer does EVERY 
 
 Werebear or Wolf build need to 
 
 Shael or 15ias every single weapon to ensure you'll hit the final breakpoints, you can add 
 
 Lo , 
 
 Ber , Facets, etc. to obtain more power.

Werebear hits 7fpa with 3os 
 
 Aldur's Rhythm shaeled up to 90% IAS and 30% non-weapon ias (2x jewels of fervor put into 
 
 Aldur's Stony Gaze , for example) - this alone turns your 
 
 Fire Claws into very fast and deadly weapon. 
 But even 2os A's R, being shaeled just to 70% IAS, hits 9fpa alone without non-weapon IAS, thus allowing wearing 
 
 Magefist and putting 2x fire facets into headgear instead

title:
Aldur's Rhythm 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected 
                                  Set Item
                                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
It's really nice that it can be upgraded to 
 
 Devil Star . And also worth noting, with the latest "attack speed" calculation in 2.6 it isn't based on the weapon speed which completely invalidates some of the posts from 4+ years ago. Like 90% of your speed is going to be based on 
 
 Maul level and weapon type, adding Shaels to your weapon only lets you require less 
 
 Maul charges but if you have a level 30+ 
 
 Maul you'll hit your max speed no matter what. This allows you to add like 3x 
 
 Ber or 2xBer, 1x 
 
 Lo in your upped 
 
 Devil Star which is really nice. You're not going to be doing Grief levels of damage, but with a Fireclaw/ 
 
 Maul Hybrid you can hit some 14-15k fireclaw and several thousand physical damge. Worth using now.

2

8

Data contributed by Stormlash

2 
 Description

Although the Angelic Raiment is comprised of a 4-peice set, the 
 
 Angelic Halo (ring) and the 
 
 Angelic Wings (Amulet) are by far the most commonly used pieces, utilized for the outstanding 2-piece bonus to Attack Rating. It is not uncommon for melee builds in both PVM and PVE to even use 2x Angelic rings + Amulet to ensure their attacks hit as often as possible. 
 
 While less common, the Magic Find granted from wearing more of the set is still useful, especially at lower levels. For a pure MF build, there are certainly better options available, however.

(Armor and Weapon visible)

title:
Angelic Halo 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected 
                                  Set Item
                                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Data contributed by Stormlash

2 
 Description

Although part of a 4 piece set, the 
 
 Angelic Halo is most commonly used in conjunction with 
 
 Angelic Wings (Amulet) for the outstanding 2-piece boost to Attack Rating. It is not uncommon for melee builds in both PvM and PvP to use 2x Angelic rings + Amulet to ensure their attacks hit as often as possible. 
 
 While less common, the +50% Magic Find granted from wearing 3 pieces is still nice, especially at lower levels. For a pure MF build, there are certainly better options available, however.

title:
Blizzard 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Skill
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Glacial Spike +5% Cold Damage per Level

Data contributed by Stormlash

2 
 Description

Summons massive shards of ice to destroy your enemies. 
 
 Casting Delay: 1.8 seconds 
 
 Blizzard causes falling ice shards to strike random size 2 areas within a 4.6 yard radius for the listed cold damage. One shard is generated every 4 frames for a total duration of 4 seconds. 25 shards are generated in total. Each shard persists for a short duration (the duration of the "falling" animation) and is subject to the Last Collide mechanic. 
 
 Blizzard requires a certain playstyle since its damage is dealt over several seconds to an area, instead of in a more targeted shot like most Sorceress spells. 
 
 Monsters will need to be herded together, or a tank used to get them to hold still while Blizzard rains down its damage.

Description by Aelanwyr

5 
 Lore

This is the most effective offensive spell that this discipline has to offer.

With the invocation of this spell, entire hordes of enemies are left chilled or dead, drowned in a hail of ice.

title:
Amplify Damage 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Skill
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Amplify Damage lowers monster Physical Resistances by 100%. It will also remove the Immune to Physical on Monsters.

Amplify Damage allows you to quickly finish enemies off. You find 
 
 Amplify Damage useful on any monsters you or your minions want to kill fast, such as Unique Bosses, Champions, Monsters that raise other Monsters such as 
 
 Mummy , or any monsters that might give you a hard time. 
 
 Use 
 
 Amplify Damage in parties with other players and in situations where you're doing more damage to the enemy than they are doing to you. 
 
 Does 
 
 Amplify Damage work on spell damage as well as physical damage? 
 No, only Physical Damage. 
 
 Does 
 
 Amplify Damage work with 
 
 Corpse Explosion ? 
 Partially. 
 
 Corpse Explosion does 50% Physical damage and 50% Fire damage.

Description by Stormlash

5 
 Lore

This deceptively potent curse rapidly advances the age and putridity of any wound.

Ordinary blows will cut through flesh and carve particularly vicious wounds that fester and seethe. 
 
 5

This is typically considered a "1 point wonder", in that additional points only increase radius, and not the amount of damage amplification. Therefore, every necromancer build only puts one point into it, and any additional points are provided by +skill gear and charms.

text:
Every necro build needs this curse. That one skill point works wonders for this, same with 
 
 Corpse Explosion . I've run no/low curse skill point builds, but I never skimp at least +1 on amplify at least. Big payoff for such a small skill point investment. +Skill items buff it by increasing cast radius.

crocorinho

4 years ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post1945910.html?sid=af855a3371241a9162f31de890e42cec#p1945910 
 
 4 years ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post1945910.html?sid=af855a3371241a9162f31de890e42cec#p1945910

In Diablo 2 Monster Physical Resistance is capped at 100%. 
 
 Curses work at 1/5 efficiency when breaking immunity. That means: 
 
 All monster's Physical Immunities can be broken by 
 
 Amplify Damage . 
 
 The resulting Physical Resistance of said monsters will be exactly 80% 
 The 
 
 Sanctuary Aura sets the Physical Resistance of monsters to 0. 
 
 Amplify Damage is apllied before Sanctuary so when using the latter it is pointless to use 
 
 Amplify Damage .

3

7

 Bone Break in your inventory, both 
 
 Amplify Damage and 
 
 Decrepify work at 100% effectiveness .

 Lower Resist and 
 
 Conviction , which only operate at 20% effectiveness on sundered enemies.

title:
Amplify Damage 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Skill
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
An addendum to the comment above - monster physical damage resistance will never naturally be higher than 100%. 
 
 However, adding the Stone Skin mod CAN increase this percentage to over 100%. 
 
 This becomes apparent if, for example, you run into an elite pack of Blood Lords with the Stone Skin mod, as you may notice that Decrep or Amp does not remove their physical damage immunity in such cases. Because both these curses work at 1/5th effectiveness without the use of 
 
 Bone Break , in such cases only reducing their physical resistance by 10 or 20% respectively still leaves them at >100%. This also applies to variants whose physical resist is 75% buffed by Stone Skin up to 125%. 
 
 This is uncommon as there aren't a ton of monsters in the game whose base physical resist is higher than 50%, but there are a handful and this can be a problematic when it happens.

2

8

title:
Arctic Blast 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Skill
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Aided by the will of the North Winds, the Druid conjures up a chilling torrent of frost that incapacitates all caught within the frozen blast. 
 
 Minimum Mana Required to Cast: 4 
 
 The Druid can cast this for as long as he likes, pivoting and aiming it after the cursor, just as the Sorceress can with 
 
 Inferno . 
 
 The chill time is quite long, so flashing targets with this will keep them slowed for a good duration. 
 
 (Note, actual damage is 1/3 listed damage.)

title:
Arctic Blast 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Skill
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Aided by the will of the North Winds, the Druid conjures up a chilling torrent of frost that incapacitates all caught within the frozen blast. 
 
 Minimum Mana Required to Cast: 4 
 
 The Druid can cast this for as long as he likes, pivoting and aiming it after the cursor, just as the Sorceress can with 
 
 Inferno . 
 
 The chill time is quite long, so flashing targets with this will keep them slowed for a good duration. 
 
 (Note, actual damage is 1/3 listed damage.)

 Arctic Blast Oskill.

Data contributed by Stormlash

2 
 Description

This terrible force of nature's vengeance rains down flaming stones around the Druid who cast it, pummeling any opponents foolish enough to be caught in its Fury .

title:
Armageddon 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Skill
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Armageddon works a little differently than expected. Once cast, it follows you around, striking random 4 yard (3 prior to Patch 2.4) radii within an 11.3 yard radius around the caster. Targets in a square at the time of a strike take the listed physical and fire damage, and a 2 yard radius centered on the same location burns with a fire Damage-over-Time effect for 36 frames. This fire DoT dealt 1/3 the listed amound in Legacy; it is unknown at this time if the issue has been resolved in Resurrected. 
 
 Prior to Patch 2.4, 
 
 Armageddon caused one strike every 8 frames. In 2.4 and beyond, this has been increased to approximately one strike every 6 frames. 
 
 To hit monsters, it's best to move in circles around the monsters until 
 
 Armageddon hits them. As you get more experienced in using 
 
 Armageddon , you will better judge how to position your character to get the best use from 
 
 Armageddon .

Armageddon works pretty well with 
 
 Tornado . You can cast a 
 
 Tornado while you're waiting to cast another 
 
 Armageddon . 
 
 You can cast 
 
 Armageddon while in 
 
 Werewolf or 
 
 Werebear form.

Description by Aelanwyr

Armageddon 
 Casting Delay has been removed for this skill 
 
 Physical Damage significantly increased 
 
 Tooltip updated to now display Physical Damage

 Removed the 
 
 Hurricane skill requirement 
 
 Increased the missile damage radius by 33% (from 3 to 4) 
 
 Increased the missile drop rate by about 30% (from ~3 per second to ~4)"

 Description

title:
Attract 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Skill
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Curses a monster to become the target of all nearby monsters. This curse may not be overridden by another curse. 
 
 Player vs Player - This will not work on other players.

Attract will not work on Super Unique Monsters and Bosses. 
 
 The 
 
 Attract curse is handy for turning monsters on one another, but cannot be cast on a boss or any super Uniques. This is great for creating your own monster battles. Watch the monsters fight it out among themselves! 
 
 You receive experience if a monster with 
 
 Attract dies, even if killed by other hostile monsters.

Attract will not overwrite 
 
 Confuse . 
 
 Attracted Monsters can still be damaged (unlike Paladin's 
 
 Conversion ) and will still attack the Necromancer or other players.

Description by Stormlash

5 
 Lore

This hex causes hallucinations in enemies which force them to stop whatever activity they were engaged in and aid the Necromancer in his attacks.

Battling their former allies with an unnatural ferocity, this is a vastly demoralizing turn of events for the enemy. 
 
 5 
 Can be used to make Runewords:

7

6 months ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post4028290.html?sid=5808aadb7f9cfb8f1eaee9ba8c330ba9#p4028290 
 
 6 months ago (pre-Resurrected) https://diablo2.io/post4028290.html?sid=5808aadb7f9cfb8f1eaee9ba8c330ba9#p4028290

Love this skill for its crowd control. Cast it on a group of monsters and you lock them in place, especially in hell, where they can't kill each other due to high HP. You can add another curse on the group after you cast this, because the "attracted" monster cannot be overridden. 
 Unfortunately, doesn't work on blue elites. it will work on unique elites' minions.

title:
Avoid 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Skill
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Passive - You have a chance to 
 
 Dodge enemy missiles when attacking or standing still. 
 
 Avoid will work while attacking. 
 
 You probably don't want to put more than a few points into Avoid. The benefit of additional points drops off significantly.

Description by Stormlash

5 
 Lore

This ability hones the natural defensive ability of an Amazon.

If she stands still, she can predict incoming missile attacks and elude them before they reach her.

Most Amazon people can do this if all of their thoughts to the task, but only a warrior trained in this skill can do this even whilst caught unaware. 
 
 5 
 Can be used to make Runewords:

7

title:
Axe Mastery 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Skill
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Passive - Improves axe fighting skill. 
 
 When a throwing axe is used, this mastery is applied only to melee attacks.

Description by Trang Oul

5 
 Lore

Barbarian warriors of the Shadow Wolf Tribe are masters of the axe.

Through the axe, they sought to match the swiping claws and the biting teeth of the wolves with which they lived and fought beside.

These first axes were but crude stones mounted on wooden hafts, and after learning the secret of steel and swords from Bul-Kathos, the axe soon fell into disfavor.

Recently, however, warriors and smiths from the Steppes have perfected the axe as a weapon equal to or greater than the sword.

The Wolf Tribe once again teaches the swift and terrible power of the axe to those who wish to learn how to cull the weak from the herd and to protect their pack from the legions of Hell. 
 
 5 
 Can be used to make Runewords:

7

Bash 
 
 Barbarian Skill 
 
 Requires level: 1 
 Skill tree: Combat Skills (Barbarian) 
 
 A powerful smashing blow that knocks the target back. 
 View skill stats 
 
 Synergies:

Stun +5% Damage per Level

Concentrate +5% Attack Rating per Level

Data contributed by Stormlash

2 
 Description

title:
Bash 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Skill
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Powerful blow that increases the damage done to enemies and knocks them back. 
 
 Monsters can be " 
 
 Bash -locked" with a quick weapon; effectively stunning them so they never get to fight back.

Bash will not knock back monsters without clear space behind them, another monster, a wall, or even objects like treasure chests and rocks will stop knockback from taking place. 
 
 None of the Act Bosses may be knocked back.

Description by Khegan

5 
 Lore

The immense physical strength of the Barbarian people is widely known, so it should come as no surprise that this is one of the first skills that they develop as a warrior.

Summoning up their renowned brute strength a Barbarian can deliver a powerful and painful blow that staggers an enemy from its feet, knocking them back. 
 
 5 
 Can be used to make Runewords:

7

 Barbarian Skill 
 
 Requires level: 30 
 Skill tree: Warcries 
 
 Temporarily increases the skills of party members by 1. 
 View skill stats

Howl

Shout

Battle Orders

title:
Battle Command 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Skill
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
Data contributed by Stormlash

2 
 Description

Increases all current skills levels for you and your party.

Battle Command increases all of your skills and the skills of party members by one. 
 
 Battle Command will make you a party favorite, so offer this skill to your party members whenever possible. This will increase the effectiveness of your whole party in most situations. 
 
 Placing additional points into 
 
 Battle Command will only lengthen its duration, so strongly consider investing skill points in 
 
 Shout or 
 
 Battle Orders instead, which also increase 
 
 Battle Command 's duration as synergies, but those skills' effects also improve with skill level. 
 
 After casting 
 
 Battle Command , cast it again. It will also increase 
 
 Battle Command skill level, so casting this twice will give you a longer duration.

Description by Trang Oul

5 
 Lore

Using this skill a Barbarian can examine the abilities of his companions and, during battle, determine how best the group should apply their various skills.

title:
Battle Command 
        
        
        
                                  • Diablo 2 Resurrected Skill
                          
        
         • diablo2.io
text:
It is this skill as well as their natural abilities as leaders that are gradually shedding the long held stereotype that the Barbarian people are merely ignorant savages.
